title: Woodsiopsis obtusa
intro: Woodsiopsis obtusa, also known as the obtuse wood fern, is a species of fern native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Woodsiopsis obtusa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Woodsiopsis obtusa is classified as a species of fern within the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is part of the genus Woodsiopsis, which contains several species of ferns.
